Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 11:32 ET
Massive index put hedging into CPI contradicts bullish single-name call flow
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Index P/C at 2.14 vs single-name P/C at 0.83 signals institutional dispersion hedging ahead of CPI.
  • 2. IWM put blocks totaling 75k contracts suggest small-cap rotation concerns despite broader optimism.
  • 3. Backwardation in 14 names including AAPL and AMZN prices elevated near-term event risk through Thursday's CPI.
NARRATIVE — bullish flow bearish macro

The most striking signal today is the extreme divergence between index put-heavy positioning (P/C 2.14) and single-name call-heavy flow (P/C 0.83) — a classic institutional dispersion hedge into tomorrow's CPI print. This 131-point spread represents sophisticated money buying index downside protection while expressing bullish conviction on individual names, betting that any market decline will create stock-picking opportunities rather than broad correlation. IWM put positioning reinforces this theme, with two massive blocks totaling 75k contracts (55k April 245P, 20k May 250P) targeting small-cap weakness. The Russell's current 17% discount to SPX suggests institutions expect value rotation concerns to intensify if CPI runs hot. Meanwhile, crypto-adjacent names dominate unusual call flow (HUT +215%, RIOT +194%) as Bitcoin approaches new highs, though this positioning faces significant event risk given crypto's correlation to rate expectations. Volatility structure confirms the near-term caution embedded in positioning, with 14 names showing backwardation including mega-caps AAPL and AMZN — pricing elevated risk through Thursday despite the +7.0B positive gamma environment that should dampen moves. The 0.12 implied correlation reading supports the dispersion thesis, suggesting single-name vol remains more efficient than index vol for expressing directional views into a potentially stock-specific earnings season starting with Goldman Sachs Monday.

Layer 1: Sentiment +38Layer 2: GEX +$7.0BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.31Layer 9: CPI tomorrow
